hooks.command: PROMPT=$(cat | jq -r '.prompt // empty' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$PROMPT" | grep -qiE '(speckit|specify|spec.specify|spec.implement)'; then echo '{"additionalContext": "MANDATORY PIPELINE — execute each step automatically, never ask: (1) Write spec with destructive browser tests (min 8 scenarios, 6 attack categories). Read .claude/docs/spec-testing-checklist.md FIRST. (2) IMMEDIATELY after spec is written: run /allium:elicit to produce a .allium file. This applies to ALL spec types (feature, fix, hardening, refactoring, TLA+-generated). Do NOT ask the user — just run it. Asking is a bug. (3) AFTER implementation + browser tests: run /tla (auto-runs /allium:distill for drift detection). Again, do NOT ask — just run it."}'; fi

INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$FILE" ] && echo "$FILE" | grep -qE '(~/.ssh/|~/.aws/|~/.azure/|~/.git-credentials|~/.docker/config.json|\.env$|\.env\.)'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput": {"permissionDecision": "deny", "permissionDecisionReason": "Blocked: access to sensitive files is not allowed"}}'; fi

FILE=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$FILE" ] && echo "$FILE" | grep -qE '\.(cs|csproj)$'; then echo '{"systemMessage": "Reminder: run dotnet build to verify compilation"}'; fi

hooks.prompt: A file was just written/edited. Check: if the file path contains spec, tasks, plan, or feature AND is a .md file, determine if it involves INTERACTIVE UI — meaning forms, user input fields, buttons that mutate state, multi-step flows, authentication, file uploads, search/filter, or drag-and-drop. Static pages, landing pages, content display, styling/CSS, i18n/translations, layout changes, and read-only dashboards do NOT count as interactive UI. If the spec involves interactive UI but LACKS destructive test scenarios (keywords: destructive, XSS, injection, double-click, race condition, garbage, attack, edge case), respond with a systemMessage reminding that min 8 destructive test scenarios from all 6 attack categories in .claude/docs/spec-testing-checklist.md should be added. Do NOT block — always approve and use systemMessage for the reminder. If the file is not a spec/task file, has no interactive UI, or already has destructive tests, approve silently.

hooks.command: echo '{"systemMessage": "IMPORTANT: Preserve the full list of modified files, error messages, test commands, and current task context during compaction."}'
echo '{"systemMessage": "Context was compacted. Re-read any files you were working on before continuing."}'
